events.title: First Crusade
events.description: European crusaders travel to the Levant and capture Jerusalem.

events.title: Henry I of England reigns
events.description: Henry I consolidates Norman rule and reforms administration.

events.title: Knights Templar founded
events.description: Military order founded to protect pilgrims in the Holy Land.

events.title: David I of Scotland reforms
events.description: David I strengthens royal authority and modernizes Scottish institutions.

events.title: Second Crusade
events.description: European kings campaign in the Levant and Iberia; largely unsuccessful.

events.title: Murder of Thomas Becket
events.description: Archbishop of Canterbury killed; sparks conflict between church and crown.

events.title: Battle of Hattin
events.description: Saladin defeats Crusader forces and captures Jerusalem.

events.title: Genghis Khan proclaimed
events.description: Temüjin unites Mongol tribes and begins empire-building.

events.title: Magna Carta sealed
events.description: Charter limiting royal power and protecting baronial rights in England.

events.title: Mongol conversions and rule
events.description: Mongol successor states consolidate across Eurasia.

events.title: Sack of Baghdad
events.description: Mongol forces destroy Baghdad, ending the Abbasid Caliphate's political power.

events.title: Marco Polo travels to Asia
events.description: Venetian merchant travels the Silk Road and visits the Yuan court.

events.title: Ottoman Beylik founded
events.description: Ottoman state emerges in Anatolia and expands into the Balkans.

events.title: Mali Empire rises
events.description: Mali becomes a powerful West African empire controlling trade routes.

events.title: Black Death pandemic
events.description: Bubonic plague spreads across Eurasia, causing massive mortality and social change.

events.title: Maritime trade in Indian Ocean
events.description: Expansion of trade networks across the Indian Ocean linking Africa, Arabia, India, and Southeast Asia.

events.title: Timurid Empire begins
events.description: Timur (Tamerlane) establishes a Central Asian empire with campaigns across West and South Asia.

events.title: Rise of Moscow
events.description: Moscow consolidates power in northeastern Rus and resists Mongol rule.

events.title: Battle of Agincourt
events.description: English victory over French during the Hundred Years' War.

events.title: Joan of Arc executed
events.description: Joan of Arc executed; later becomes a symbol of French nationalism.

events.title: Fall of Constantinople
events.description: Ottoman forces capture Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire.

events.title: Printing press spreads
events.description: Movable type printing spreads in Europe, accelerating information exchange.

events.title: Columbus reaches the Americas
events.description: Christopher Columbus lands in the Caribbean, initiating sustained contact between Europe and the Americas.

events.title: Treaty of Tordesillas
events.description: Spain and Portugal divide newly discovered lands outside Europe.

events.title: Aztec Empire at height
events.description: Triple Alliance dominates central Mexico until Spanish conquest.

events.title: Martin Luther posts Theses
events.description: Protestant Reformation begins, challenging Catholic Church practices.

events.title: Cortés conquers Aztecs
events.description: Spanish conquest leads to fall of Tenochtitlan and Aztec Empire.

events.title: Babur founds Mughal Empire
events.description: Babur defeats Ibrahim Lodi at Panipat and establishes Mughal rule in India.

events.title: Spanish conquest of the Inca
events.description: Pizarro captures Atahualpa and begins Spanish control over the Inca Empire.

events.title: Copernican heliocentrism
events.description: Nicolaus Copernicus publishes heliocentric model, challenging geocentric cosmology.

events.title: Defeat of the Spanish Armada
events.description: English fleet defeats the Spanish Armada, shifting naval power in Europe.

events.title: Tokugawa shogunate established
events.description: Tokugawa Ieyasu establishes stable shogunate and long period of isolation in Japan.

events.title: Jamestown founded
events.description: First permanent English settlement in North America.

events.title: Thirty Years' War begins
events.description: Religious and political conflict across Central Europe with massive devastation.

events.title: Qing dynasty established
events.description: Manchu forces establish Qing rule over China.

events.title: Glorious Revolution in England
events.description: William of Orange takes the English throne; constitutional changes follow.

events.title: Act of Union (England and Scotland)
events.description: England and Scotland unite to form Great Britain.

events.title: Seven Years' War
events.description: Global conflict among European powers with theaters in Europe, North America, India, and elsewhere.

events.title: Early Industrial Revolution
events.description: Mechanization and factory production begin in Britain and spread to Europe and North America.

events.title: American Declaration of Independence
events.description: Thirteen American colonies declare independence from Britain.

events.title: French Revolution begins
events.description: Revolutionary upheaval that overthrows the monarchy and reshapes Europe.

events.title: Napoleon rises to power
events.description: Napoleon Bonaparte becomes First Consul and later Emperor, leading European campaigns.

events.title: Haitian Revolution and independence
events.description: Slave revolt leads to independence of Haiti, first Black republic in the modern era.

events.title: Congress of Vienna
events.description: European powers reorganize the continent after Napoleonic Wars to restore balance.

events.title: First Opium War
events.description: Conflict between Qing China and Britain over trade and sovereignty; Hong Kong ceded to Britain.

events.title: Revolutions of 1848
events.description: Widespread liberal and nationalist uprisings across Europe.

events.title: Commodore Perry opens Japan
events.description: US expedition forces Japan to end isolation and open ports to foreign trade.

events.title: Indian Rebellion of 1857
events.description: Large-scale uprising against the British East India Company's rule; leads to direct Crown rule.

events.title: American Civil War
events.description: Conflict between Union and Confederacy over slavery and union.

events.title: Meiji Restoration
events.description: Restoration of imperial rule in Japan and rapid modernization.

events.title: German Empire proclaimed
events.description: Proclamation of the German Empire under Prussian leadership at Versailles.

events.title: Berlin Conference
events.description: European powers formalize the partition of Africa during the Scramble for Africa.

events.title: First modern Olympic Games
events.description: Revival of the Olympic Games in Athens, promoting international sport.

events.title: Boxer Rebellion
events.description: Anti-foreign uprising in China suppressed by an international coalition.

events.title: Russo-Japanese War
events.description: Conflict over influence in Manchuria and Korea; Japanese victory marks rise of Japan.

events.title: Xinhai Revolution
events.description: Revolution ends Qing dynasty and leads to the Republic of China.

events.title: World War I
events.description: Global conflict centered in Europe involving major world powers and industrial warfare.

events.title: Russian Revolution
events.description: Bolshevik Revolution leads to collapse of the Russian Empire and rise of the Soviet state.

events.title: Spanish flu pandemic
events.description: Influenza pandemic causing tens of millions of deaths worldwide after World War I.

events.title: Treaty of Versailles
events.description: Peace treaty that formally ended World War I and imposed terms on Germany.

events.title: League of Nations founded
events.description: International organization aimed at preventing future wars, precursor to the UN.

events.title: Northern Expedition
events.description: Nationalist campaign to unify China under the Kuomintang.

events.title: Great Depression begins
events.description: Severe global economic downturn triggered by the US stock market crash.

events.title: Japanese invasion of Manchuria
events.description: Japan seizes Manchuria and establishes the puppet state of Manchukuo.

events.title: Spanish Civil War
events.description: Conflict between Republicans and Nationalists; prelude to wider ideological struggles in Europe.

events.title: World War II
events.description: Largest global war in history with theaters across Europe, Asia, Africa, and the Pacific.

events.title: United Nations founded
events.description: International organization established to promote peace and cooperation after World War II.

events.title: India and Pakistan gain independence
events.description: British India partitioned into India and Pakistan; independence achieved amid mass migration and violence.

events.title: People's Republic of China proclaimed
events.description: Chinese Communist Party proclaims the PRC after victory in the civil war.

events.title: Korean War
events.description: Conflict on the Korean Peninsula between North and South Korea with international involvement.

events.title: Sputnik launched
events.description: Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite, beginning the space age.

events.title: Cuban Revolution
events.description: Revolutionary movement led by Fidel Castro overthrows Batista and establishes a socialist state.

events.title: Civil Rights Act (USA)
events.description: Major US legislation outlawing discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.

events.title: Apollo 11 Moon landing
events.description: NASA's Apollo 11 mission lands humans on the Moon for the first time.

events.title: Bangladesh independence
events.description: East Pakistan becomes independent Bangladesh after war with Pakistan.

events.title: 1973 Oil Crisis
events.description: OPEC oil embargo triggers global energy crisis and economic disruption.

events.title: Iranian Revolution
events.description: Revolution overthrows the Shah and establishes the Islamic Republic under Ayatollah Khomeini.

events.title: Neoliberal economic reforms
events.description: Market-oriented reforms in the UK, US, and elsewhere reshape global economics.

events.title: Chernobyl nuclear disaster
events.description: Catastrophic nuclear accident in Soviet Ukraine with long-term health and environmental effects.

events.title: Fall of the Berlin Wall
events.description: Symbolic end of Cold War divisions as the Berlin Wall is opened and later dismantled.

events.title: Tiananmen Square protests
events.description: Pro-democracy protests in Beijing culminating in a government crackdown.

events.title: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
events.description: The USSR formally dissolves into independent republics, ending the Cold War superpower structure.

events.title: Rwandan Genocide
events.description: Mass killings of Tutsi and moderate Hutu in Rwanda with catastrophic human toll.

events.title: Handover of Hong Kong
events.description: Sovereignty over Hong Kong transfers from the UK to the People's Republic of China.

events.title: 9/11 terrorist attacks
events.description: Coordinated terrorist attacks on the United States with major global security consequences.

events.title: Iraq War begins
events.description: US-led invasion topples Saddam Hussein; long-term conflict and occupation follow.

events.title: EU enlargement (2004)
events.description: Ten countries join the European Union in its largest single expansion.

events.title: Global Financial Crisis
events.description: Severe worldwide economic downturn triggered by a US housing market collapse and banking failures.

events.title: Arab Spring uprisings
events.description: Series of anti-government protests and uprisings across the Arab world with varied outcomes.

events.title: Fukushima nuclear disaster
events.description: Earthquake and tsunami trigger nuclear accidents in Japan with long-term impacts.

events.title: Annexation of Crimea
events.description: Russia annexes Crimea from Ukraine, triggering international sanctions and tensions.

events.title: Rise of ISIS
events.description: Islamic State declares a caliphate across parts of Iraq and Syria, prompting international military responses.

events.title: Brexit referendum
events.description: United Kingdom votes to leave the European Union; complex withdrawal process follows.

events.title: COVID-19 pandemic
events.description: Global pandemic caused by SARS-CoV-2 with major health, social, and economic impacts.

events.title: Global protests for racial justice
events.description: Widespread protests against racial injustice and police violence sparked by incidents in the United States.

events.title: Full-scale invasion of Ukraine
events.description: Russia launches a large-scale invasion of Ukraine, leading to ongoing conflict and global repercussions.

events.title: Record climate extremes and policy focus
events.description: Increasing frequency of extreme weather events and growing global climate policy action.

events.title: Rapid advances in generative AI
events.description: Widespread adoption and rapid development of large-scale generative AI systems reshaping industries and policy debates.

events.title: Post-pandemic economic adjustments
events.description: Global economies adjust to post-pandemic realities with inflation, supply-chain shifts, and policy changes.

events.title: Major elections and geopolitical realignments
events.description: Significant elections and shifting alliances reshape regional and global politics.

events.title: New era of commercial space missions
events.description: Commercial and national space missions expand human activity in low Earth orbit and beyond.
